frameworks.name: cortical oscillations / cross-frequency coordination
frameworks.one_line: low- and high-frequency rhythms jointly organize neural activity into whole-brain functional networks

frameworks.name: BOLD-based functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: fMRI networks reinterpreted as summed neural activity of transient oscillation-defined configurations

frameworks.name: local-vs-distant frequency-scale mapping
frameworks.one_line: the simple assignment of high frequencies to local and low frequencies to long-range coordination is treated as oversimplified

core_question: How do patterns of cortical oscillations across frequencies relate to whole-brain functional networks observed in the fMRI BOLD signal?
key_assumptions: EEG/MEG oscillations and BOLD networks index a common underlying neural organization
cognitive/network states change on sub-second timescales
BOLD temporally integrates faster neural events
key_findings: high- and low-frequency oscillations operate in concert across spatial scales rather than in a strict local/distant dichotomy
cortical oscillation patterns reconfigure on cognitive timescales (often under one second)
interpretations: BOLD functional networks likely reflect the temporal summation of multiple transient oscillation-defined network configurations
joint analysis of fast oscillations and slow BOLD is needed to characterize whole-brain functional networks
